Union.name: Union Station
Union.description: You are in Union Station, the busiest transit hub in Canada, connecting GO Transit, VIA Rail, and the TTC. The crowd moves endlessly around you. TMU station is NorthEast and Museum station is NorthWest.

items.name: PRESTO CARD
items.description: A small black card used to pay for transit. Funds are limited.

TMU.name: TMU Station
TMU.description: You step out into the busy downtown core near Toronto Metropolitan University. Students rush past, and the city hums with energy. Streetcars rattle nearby.

items.name: TMU ID CARD
items.description: Your student ID card for TMU.

items.name: Screwdriver
items.description: A small tool that could be useful in the future.

Bloor-Yonge.name: Bloor-Yonge Station
Bloor-Yonge.description: You are currently in Canada Busiest Subway Station. From here you can connect to Line 2 Bloor-Danforth. East to Kennedy and West to Kipling.

Eglinton.name: Eglinton Station
Eglinton.description: You are currently in Toronto's newest subway station with connection to Line 5 Eglinton. You can go east to Kennedy and west to Mount Dennis.

Museum.name: Museum Station
Museum.description: You are in Museum Station, a quiet stop on the TTC line. There are artifacts and totem poles on display, and the station has a calm atmosphere.

items.name: Artifact
items.description: An ancient artifact on display at the museum.

Kipling.name: Kipling Station
Kipling.description: You are in Kipling Station, the station is quiet and the air is filled with the scent of fresh coffee from a nearby café. The station is a hub for commuters heading to the airport and the suburbs.

Dundas West.name: Dundas West Station
Dundas West.description: You are in Dundas West Station, a busy stop on the TTC line. The station is known for its vibrant atmosphere and diverse crowd.

items.name: Crowbar
items.description: A sturdy crowbar that could be useful for prying open stuck objects.
